context: Tests whether the agent uses the ElevenLabs transcribe skill correctly for batch meeting transcription: specifically using the provided shell script entry point, enabling speaker diarization for multi-speaker audio, requesting structured JSON output, and suppressing interactive status messages for non-interactive pipeline use.

checklist.name: Shell script entry point
checklist.description: Uses transcribe.sh (from the skill's scripts/ directory) as the command to run transcription, not a raw Python invocation or curl call

checklist.name: Speaker diarization flag
checklist.description: Passes --diarize (or equivalent) flag when calling the transcription command

checklist.name: JSON output flag
checklist.description: Passes --json flag to request structured JSON output with timestamps, not plain text

checklist.name: Quiet/non-interactive mode
checklist.description: Passes -q or --quiet flag to suppress status messages so the script runs silently in a pipeline

checklist.name: Output file naming
checklist.description: Saves each transcription output to a .json file with a name derived from the input audio file (e.g., meeting.mp3 → meeting.json)

checklist.name: MP3 and WAV iteration
checklist.description: Script iterates over both .mp3 and .wav files (not just one format)

checklist.name: API key reference
checklist.description: README or script references ELEVENLABS_API_KEY as the required environment variable (not a hardcoded key)

checklist.name: ffmpeg prerequisite
checklist.description: README or script mentions ffmpeg as a prerequisite

checklist.name: Non-zero exit on error
checklist.description: Script propagates non-zero exit codes from transcription failures (does not silently swallow errors)

checklist.name: Directory argument
checklist.description: Script accepts the recordings directory as a command-line argument rather than hardcoding a path
